mnemonics: Remember the '5' in Module 5 stands for the '5' key points to review for your Continuous Internal Evaluation (CIE).
keyPoints: Understand the core operations (Insertion, Deletion, Traversal) for data structures covered.
Be able to compare and contrast different data structures (e.g., Arrays vs. Linked Lists, Stacks vs. Queues).
Know the real-world applications of each data structure (e.g., stacks for undo operations, queues for scheduling).
Practice implementing the data structures in pseudocode or a programming language.
Focus on the time and space complexity (Big-O notation) of common operations.
commonMistakes: Confusing the LIFO (Last-In-First-Out) principle of a stack with the FIFO (First-In-First-Out) principle of a queue.
Incorrectly calculating time complexity by ignoring nested loops or recursive calls.
Using an inappropriate data structure for a given problem scenario, leading to inefficient solutions.
